div.head: The Chargé in Great Britain (Atherton) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 166. Department’s 147, May 26, 2 p.m. For the Department of Agriculture from Olsen.

“Countries represented Conference not recognizing fundamentals in solution wheat problem. Since clearing house would have creation participating nations believe it promising vehicle to obtain additional data such as port unloads, stocks requirements, also improve statistical economic services participating nations and influence production marketing policies exporting countries. No assurance can induce Russia supply additional statistics but clearly may uncover additional Russian information. Institute Agriculture not functioning adequately. Clearing house may stimulate reforms. Our foreign services can secure much valuable data but not in as strategic position as clearing house to influence activities other nations. Great effort at Conference to place American delegation in negative position. Clearing house purely informational and educational and believe our participating desirable.”
